title: Risk factors for death in patients with sepsis admitted to an obstetric intensive care unit: A cohort study

sample_size.source_quote: The remaining 155 patients, representing 5.1% of all admissions to the ICU, were included in the analysis.

patient_age_mean.source_quote: The mean age of the patients who died was 27.1 years (SD=6.8), signiﬁcantly higher than the mean age of those who survived (23.6 years, SD=6.6) (P=.02).

sofa_score_baseline.source_quote: The median SOFA score at the time of diagnosis was 6 (interquartile range [IQR] 4–9.5) in the patients who died and 2 (IQR 0–5) in those who survived

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: Twenty-two of the patients who developed sepsis died (14.2%)

mortality_predictors.source_quote: septic shock at the time of hospitalization (relative risk [RR]=3.45; 95% conﬁdence interval [CI]: 1.64–7.25)
need for vasopressors during hospitalization (RR= 17.32; 95% CI: 4.20–71.36)

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Septic shock at admission, lactate levels >2 mmol/L, SOFA score >2
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Following multiple logistic regression analysis, only the need for vasopressors during hospitalization remained as a risk factor associated with death (odds ratio [OR]=26.38; 95% CI: 5.87–118.51).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Lactate levels >2 mmol/L at the time of diagnosis
mortality_predictors.source_quote: lactate levels >2mmol/L at the time of diagnosis (RR=4.60; 95% CI: 1.05–20.07)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: SOFA score >2 at the time of diagnosis
mortality_predictors.source_quote: sequential organ failure assessment score >2 at the time of diagnosis (RR=5.97; 95% CI: 1.82–19.94)
urinary infection was identiﬁed as a protective factor against death in patients with sepsis (RR=0.26, 95% CI=0.08–0.85, P=.009)
